title: Horror Reading List for October 2022 - Poltergeist, We are Here to Hurt Each Other, and more
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UiD0-skn904

In this quick Hobby Cast episode, Daniel (@dungeondive) lays out the four books on his spooky-season reading list for 2022 — an invitation to read along, with reviews promised by early November. First up: the Poltergeist novelization by James Kahn. Daniel's history with novelizations is a lovely bit of backstory — as a kid he wasn't allowed to watch most horror films, but he could read anything, so he experienced the movies through their books, which were often based on shooting scripts and full of scenes the theatrical cuts dropped. He's hoping Poltergeist hides a few deleted moments. The rest of the stack: Paula D. Ashe's We Are Here to Hurt Each Other, a 2022 collection recommended by Matthew M. Bartlett that arrives wearing an intimidating content warning; Adam L.G. Nevill's Wyrd and Other Derelictions, horror told entirely through aftermaths — silent places where catastrophe has already happened and the reader must sift the ruins; and Charles L. Grant's Nightmare Seasons, set in his Oxrun Station. Grant is a writer's writer Daniel badly wants to love after a disappointing first encounter with The Gift. He's also finishing The Dead Zone, with the Ran(King) of King series set to resurrect after.

----

Daniel grew up reading novelizations in place of the films he wasn't allowed to watch. Have you ever read the book of a movie first — and did the film ever live up to the version in your head?
